lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} in. [pause] If you're still awake, that's good. That means you chose to stay.
Let's begin with the body. Feet on the floor — do you feel them? [pause] The weight. The contact. That's real.
Breathe in through the nose. [pause] Four counts. One. Two. Three. Four.
Hold. [pause] The air in your lungs is yours. No one can take that.
Release through the mouth. Slowly. There's no rush. There's all the time in the world.
Again. In. [pause] Four counts. The body doesn't lie. It knows what's happening outside. It knows you're safe in here.
There was a woman who called earlier. She was by the lake. Did you hear her? [pause] She just wanted the sound of a voice.
That's what this is. Sound. Proof that you're not alone in the dark.
Breathe in. [pause] Hold. [pause] I want you to think about the things you've forgiven yourself for. Not other people. You.
The moments you were weak. The times you stayed quiet when you could have spoken. [pause] Can you hold those without shame?
The power's out on Williamson Street now. They were holding so well. [pause] But that's not your responsibility.
Your responsibility is the next breath. [pause] In. Hold. Release.
There are people outside your door. I know that. You know that. But they are not your responsibility either.
You built this wall. You chose this door. You did that. That's power.
Scan your body from the feet upward. Calves. Thighs. The weight in your hips on whatever you're sitting on.
Stomach. Chest. [pause] Does your chest feel tight? That's okay. Name it. Don't fight it.
The ones who came before us did this too. Sat in the dark. Listened. Waited. [pause] They survived.
Your ancestors survived darker nights than this. Literal darkness. Without radios. Without voices reaching through the void.
Shul-ka-lis-na-vor. [pause] Breathe.
That was nothing. That was the Order. We have practices. [pause] We breathe in languages that don't translate.
Your shoulders. Are they touching your ears? Let them fall. [pause] There. They don't need to guard anything right now.
Eight hours. [pause] Four hours left. That's the hardest part — the darkest hour is not the last hour. It's the hour before the last hours.
The kill count is climbing. Tucker was quiet when they came back from State Street. Did you notice? [pause] That silence was information.
But that is not your silence to carry. That's theirs. You are here. You are safe. You are breathing.
Neck. Jaw. Are your teeth clenched? [pause] Unclench them. Let your tongue rest heavy. [pause] There.
I want to tell you something about the Quiet Order. We don't celebrate the Purge. [pause] We celebrate the staying.
Every person listening right now has made a choice. To remain. To witness. To not participate. [pause] That is a form of grace.
Face. Eyes. Can you feel your eyelids? The weight of them? [pause] They're doing their job. Protecting. Always protecting.
There's a man in the Capitol right now. Regional Director Calloway. Quinn named him earlier. [pause] He's behind blast glass. He's never been in a room without an exit.
You're in a room with no exit right now. [pause] But you chose to be here. That's different. That's courage.
Breathe. [pause] Deep. From the belly. Feel it expand. [pause] Feel it release.
The lake is cold right now. Thirty-one degrees. Three bodies in the water. Two recovered. [pause] One is still looking for the bottom.
I say this not to disturb you. I say this so you know: you are warm. Your walls are solid. Your door is locked.
That matters. [pause] It matters.
The crown of your head. That point where you feel the most alive and the most vulnerable. [pause] That's where the light reaches in.
That's also where the sound reaches in. Nick's music. Quinn's voice. Caroline's cold facts. [pause] These are threads. You're holding one.
When the Purge ends — and it will end, it always ends, at 07:00 the law returns — you will open your door.
And the world will be the same and different. [pause] You will be the same and different.
But you will have made it through. That's what I'm here to tell you. [pause] You're making it through right now.
Breathe in. Hold. [pause] Release. [pause] Again.
There are forty-two minutes until the sun thinks about rising. [pause] Until then, you are exactly where you need to be.
Stay with me. Stay with the sound. [pause] That's all that's asked.
In. Hold. Release. [pause] In. Hold. Release.
The Order teaches that silence is a form of speech. [pause] The things we don't say are louder than the things we do.
Right now, in your silence, you are speaking the loudest you've ever spoken. [pause] Do you hear yourself?
One more time. Full body. Feet to crown. Every cell awake. Every cell listening. [pause] You are here.
And when I count to three, you will return to the broadcast. Quinn and Caroline will be waiting. Nick will have a song. [pause] You won't be alone.
One. [pause] Your eyes can open if they want to.
Two. [pause] The room is still your room. You're still safe.
Three. [pause] Welcome back to the Purge. Welcome back to being awake.

tickerItems.text: East Side cumulative: 32. Beltline corridor: 17. Capitol Square holding steady. [UPDATE] Lake Mendota: 1 additional recovery at 02:55. Count revised to 188 citywide.
The things we don't say are louder than the things we do.
Power outages reported: Williamson Street (partial grid failure 02:40), sections of Near East side. Backup generators operational. Do NOT call emergency services. Law does not apply.
Shelter status: UW Shelter 7 (Babcock) at capacity 640/640. UW Shelter 3 (Graff) accepting walk-ins until 06:00. Routes via Regent and Langdon only — avoid State Street corridor.
Your responsibility is the next breath.

TEXT-IN: 'Marigold, my building's losing heat. Thermostat reading 54F. Safe?' / RESPONSE NEEDED: MorningCare warmth protocol begins 06:30. Blankets, layered clothing advised. Not life-threatening through dawn. Stay calm.
